title: Tempo for Cardono Governance - Maintenance & Development Budget for 2025

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this InfoAction (expired), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: evidence did not clear the directional decision threshold. Why: Pinned anchor document is available for this action. Additional context: Flag score present (3), reducing confidence. Confidence context: score 0.05; confidence 57.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Network DRep distribution used as one signal, not authority.
top_fixes: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Clarify governance intent and expected measurable outcome.
Map each major claim to primary receipts and stable source links.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Tempo for Cardono Governance - Maintenance & Development Budget for 2025
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Tempo.Vote was developed out of passion to support Cardano decentralized mission., We aim to make Cardano governance easily accessible, especially on mobile phone & wallet to ADA holder can vote on-chain anytime, anywhere., Tempo.Vote runs our own Cardano node, backend indexer independently to ensure we have the most accurate governance voting data so that we are not reliant on any 3rd party centralized provider., However this independent infrastructure comes at significant running cost.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, low materiality): Tempo.Vote was developed out of passion to support Cardano decentralized mission.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): We aim to make Cardano governance easily accessible, especially on mobile phone & wallet to ADA holder can vote on-chain anytime, anywhere.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Tempo.Vote runs our own Cardano node, backend indexer independently to ensure we have the most accurate governance voting data so that we are not reliant on any 3rd party centralized provider.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, low materiality): However this independent infrastructure comes at significant running cost.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): In order to ensure Tempo.Vote will continue running smoothly to service Cardano community, we are seeking to withdraw from Treasury 380k ADA for the 6 month period (1 Aug 2025 - 31 Jan 2026) with the following breakdown: - Infrastructure Cost: 200k ADA - De...

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): In the next 6 months, Tempo aim to deliver the following features to support Cardano Governance: - Optimize Governance Action Voting & Display - Improve UX for submitting Governance Action - Support Lending Pool to allow anyone to pay a small fee and borrow...
missing: Independent evidence for: In order to ensure Tempo.Vote will continue running smoothly to service Cardano community, we are seeking to withdraw from Treasury 380k ADA for the 6 month period (1 Aug 2025 - 31 Jan 2026) with the following breakdown: - Infrastructure Cost: 200k ADA - De...
Independent evidence for: If Approved, this budget and associated Treasury Withdrawal will be administered by Selfdriven (https://selfdriven.fyi/services)
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "We aim to make Cardano governance easily accessible, especially on mobile phone & wallet to ADA holder can vote on-chain anytime, anywhere." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "In order to ensure Tempo.Vote will continue running smoothly to service Cardano community, we are seeking to withdraw from Treasury 380k ADA for the 6 month period (1 Aug 2025 - 31 Jan 2026) with the following breakdown: - Infrastructure Cost: 200k ADA - De..." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
